# Exa

> AI Registry entry [AIR-66](https://linear.app/janusd/issue/AIR-66/exa) — status **Backlog** as of 2026-04-02. Departments: ai-office.

**Category:** AI Search API / Web Data Infrastructure
**Status:** Backlog
**Cost:** Usage-based; Free 1k req/mo; Search $5/1k; Deep Search $12/1k; Monitors $15/1k; Answer $5/1k; Contents $3/1k pages
**Departments:** AI Policy, Technology
**Entity:** Exa Labs Inc.

## Overview

AI-native web search API built for powering AI agents and applications with real-time web data. Purpose-built for programmatic use — structured, token-efficient results optimised for LLM consumption. Used by Notion, Vercel, Cursor, Databricks, AWS, HubSpot, Monday.com, Groq.

## Key Products

* **Search API** — real-time web search, 180ms latency, AI-optimised highlights
* **Deep Search** — multi-step agent workflows with structured outputs, web-grounded citations
* **Contents API** — full webpage retrieval with token-efficient highlights (50% reduction)
* **Monitors** — track web events with webhook notifications
* **Answer API** — fast web-grounded answers with citations
* **Websets** — curated datasets from web data
* **MCP Server** — native Model Context Protocol
* **Specialised Indexes** — people (1B+ profiles), companies (70M+), code/docs, financial, news

## Security

* SOC 2 Type II
* Zero Data Retention (ZDR) option
* SSO, Trust Centre at trust.exa.ai

## Benchmarks

Leads FRAMES, Tip-of-Tongue, Seal0 — outperforms Perplexity and Brave.

## Janus Fit

Foundational search infrastructure for AI initiatives. MCP integration makes it relevant for Claude agent frameworks. Structured outputs particularly relevant for consulting practice (research agents extracting specific data from web). Free tier (1k req/mo) enables PoC testing.

## Considerations

* Usage-based pricing — needs volume estimation
* API-only — requires engineering integration effort
* Overlap with Claude/Gemini built-in search
* Free tier + Startup grants ($1k free credits)

*Backlog. AI Office Infrastructure tier.*
